name: Shaykhah Rukayya Samsodien
title: Founder & Director
credential_text: Degree in Sharī'ah · Abū Nour Institute, Damascus · High Court Accredited Arabic–English Translator
bio_paragraph_1: Shaykhah Rukayya completed memorisation of the Qur'ān under the late Shaykh Mohammed Yusuf Booley. She then returned to school and completed matric at Islamia College, after which she commenced six years of study in Damascus, Syria.
bio_paragraph_2: She received a diploma in Arabic Language and Islamic Studies from Abū Nour Institute, continued at Al-Fath University in the Faculty of Sharī'ah, and holds a degree in Sharī'ah. During her time in Syria, she recited the entire Qur'ān to Shaykh Abū al-Hasan Muhayyiddīn al-Kurdī in the narration of Hafs via the Ṭāriq of al-Shāṭibiyyah and received her sanad and ijāza therein.
bio_paragraph_3: She served seven years as Principal and Hifth Consultant at Jam'Eyyatul Qurrā' Female Hifth Institute, and lectured full-time at Dārun Na'īm, Wynberg for six years. She is also an accredited Arabic–English translator of the High Court of South Africa.
publications: Hifth Guide: Assisting Your Loved Ones Along Their Hifth Journey
Muhadathah Workbook 1 — Conversational Arabic: Take the Leap to Speak
Muhadathah Workbook 2 — Conversational Arabic: Take the Leap to Speak
